English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Justin S. Morrill papers
Morrill, Justin S. (Justin Smith), 1810-1898.
1146
3.3 cubic feet. 3.3 cubic feet.
1814-1937, 1982. Correspondence, drafts of speeches, pamphlets and other printed material, scrapbooks, photographs, and other personal items. Correspondence, drafts of speeches, pamphlets and other printed material, mainly relating to the Civil War and Reconstruction, the status of freed blacks, the tariff and international trade, passage of the Land Grant College Act, and other public questions. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 2 ======= 1 2 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2083909
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
E. Engst, M.E. Warren
Date completed:
May 1990
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, April 2003
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, November 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Justin S. Morrill was a congressman and financier.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, drafts of speeches, pamphlets and other printed material, mainly relating to the Civil War and Reconstruction, the status of freed blacks, the tariff and international trade, passage of the Land Grant College Act, and other public questions; scrapbook containing a photocopy of Morrill's Wanderings and Scribblings, or A Journal of a Journey South and West in May, June and July, A.D. 1841; a scrapbook of obituary notices; a scrapbook and other items relating to the 75th anniversary of the Land Grant College Act, 1937; and photographs and other personal items. Correspondents include George T. Brown, Schuyler Colfax, John H. Freeman, James A. Garfield, Horace Greeley, Benjamin Harrison, Rutherford B. Hayes, William Dean Howells, Nellie Grant Sartoris, Jacob Gould Schurman, John Sherman, Horatio King (who wrote letters to Morrill on his birthday), A. D. White and President Grover Cleveland.
Note: Item lists of letters and speeches are available in the accession folder.
Cite As:
Justin S. Morrill papers, #1146. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Speeches. Scrapbooks. Photographs. Obituraries. United States -- History -- Civil War, 1861-1865. Legislators -- United States. Voyages and travels. Tariff. School lands. Education -- New York (State) Freedmen. Commerce. African Americans. Brown, George T. Cleveland, Grover, 1837-1908. Colfax, Schuyler, 1823-1885. Freeman, John H. Garfield, James A. (James Abram), 1831-1881. Greeley, Horace, 1811-1872. Harrison, Benjamin, 1833-1901. Hayes, Rutherford B., 1822-1893. Howells, William Dean, 1837-1920. King, Horatio, 1811-1897 Morrill, Justin S. (Justin Smith), 1810-1898. Sartoris, Nellie Grant. Schurman, Jacob Gould, 1854-1942. Sherman, John, 1823-1900. White, Andrew Dickson, 1832-1918. Morrill, Justin S. (Justin Smith), 1810-1898.
